machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Outer-membrane secretin of T3SS1 = vp1696 (commonly VscC1). Named as a T3SS1 structural component in Nydam/Call 2014 transcriptome; locus VP1696. VscN1 ATPase entry separately corroborated by Nguyen 2020.

machinery_genes.quote: vscN1 and vscN2 encoding a cytoplasmic protein of the T3SS apparatus
machinery_genes.note: VscN1 is the T3SS1 ATPase; named directly in Nguyen 2020 (mSphere). The transcriptome paper independently lists the ATPase as vp1668; both refer to the T3SS1 cytoplasmic ATPase.

machinery_genes.quote: vcrD1 (T3SS1) ... encoding the inner membrane structural ring for T3SS apparatus
machinery_genes.note: VcrD1 = the LcrD/SctV-family inner-membrane export ring of T3SS1, named in Nguyen 2020. Part of the inner-membrane export apparatus block (vp1662, vp1672-vp1675) in the Nydam/Call 2014 transcriptome.

machinery_genes.quote: the vscF gene encodes the needle protein of T3SS1 in V. parahaemolyticus
machinery_genes.note: Lian/Dai 2021 show VscF is the T3SS1 needle protein, homologous to the MxiH (paper writes 'MixH') needle of Shigella. Strain POR-1 derivative of RIMD2210633.

machinery_genes.quote: VP1657 (VopB1) and VP1656 (VopD1), which share sequence similarity with Pseudomonas genes popB (38%) and popD (36%), respectively, are essential for translocation of T3SS1 effectors into host cells ... we conclude that VopB1 and VopD1 are essential components of the translocon.
machinery_genes.note: Shimohata/Takahashi 2012; VopB1 = VP1657, PopB/YopB-family major translocator. Translocon proteins counted as apparatus per the T3SS brief (they form the host-membrane pore), not effectors.

machinery_genes.quote: VP1657 (VopB1) and VP1656 (VopD1), which share sequence similarity with Pseudomonas genes popB (38%) and popD (36%), respectively, are essential for translocation of T3SS1 effectors into host cells ... we conclude that VopB1 and VopD1 are essential components of the translocon.
machinery_genes.note: VopD1 = VP1656, PopD/YopD-family translocator. Also reported by Park 2004 as VopD, a YopD homologue secreted in a TTSS1-dependent manner. Apparatus (translocon), not effector.

machinery_genes.quote: we conclude that Vp1659 is a T3SS1-associated protein that is a component of the secretion apparatus and that it is necessary for the efficient translocation of effector proteins into epithelial cells
machinery_genes.note: Zhou/Call 2010; Vp1659 (VP1659) shares C-terminal homology with Yersinia LcrV (the needle-tip protein). Authors explicitly call it 'a component of the secretion apparatus'. Tip/translocon, not an effector.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Named ruler (SctP/YscP-family) of T3SS1 = VP1670. No separate Vsc gene name published; locus name retained as the gene identifier.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Inner rod (SctI/YscI-family) of T3SS1 = VP1691, named in Nydam/Call 2014. Locus name retained as gene identifier (no published Vsc name).

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Cytoplasmic (C-)ring (SctQ/YscQ-family) of T3SS1 = VP1671, named in Nydam/Call 2014. Locus name retained as gene identifier.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: One of the two membrane-supramembrane (MS) ring genes (SctD/SctJ-family) of T3SS1 = VP1690, named in Nydam/Call 2014. Locus name retained.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Second membrane-supramembrane (MS) ring gene (SctD/SctJ-family) of T3SS1 = VP1695, named in Nydam/Call 2014. Locus name retained.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Inner-membrane export-apparatus block of T3SS1 (SctR/SctS/SctT/SctU export-gate family) = VP1662, VP1672, VP1673, VP1674, VP1675, named collectively in Nydam/Call 2014. Listed as five separate loci; locus names retained.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Member of the inner-membrane export apparatus block; VP1672. Locus name retained.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Member of the inner-membrane export apparatus block; VP1673. Locus name retained.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Member of the inner-membrane export apparatus block; VP1674. Locus name retained.

machinery_genes.quote: Most of the T3SS1 structural genes also showed increased expression, including the ruler (vp1670), inner rod (vp1691), the inner membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675), ATPase (vp1668), cytoplasmic ring (vp1671), membrane-supramembrane ring (vp1690, vp1695), and the outer membrane secretin ring (vp1696).
machinery_genes.note: Member of the inner-membrane export apparatus block; VP1675. Locus name retained.

notes: System: T3SS1, the chromosome-1 injectisome of V. parahaemolyticus RIMD 2210633 (genes vp1656-vp1702), a Yersinia ysc/Ysc-like (Sct) injectisome that drives host-cell cytotoxicity; it injects the effectors VopQ/VP1680 and VopS/VP1686 (the curated effectors for this instance). The genome paper (Makino 2003, Lancet) first identified the T3SS gene set; Park 2004 (IAI) proved TTSS1 is a functional secretion apparatus in RIMD2210633 but referred to the apparatus genes only generically ('30 genes have sequence similarity with genes encoding the apparatus in Yersinia'), so it is cited as context, not a source of individual gene-naming quotes. Individual structural components with vp loci come from the Nydam/Call 2014 RNA-seq paper, which names the secretin (vp1696), ATPase (vp1668), inner rod (vp1691), ruler (vp1670), cytoplasmic C-ring (vp1671), the two MS-rings (vp1690, vp1695) and the five-gene inner-membrane export apparatus (vp1662, vp1672-vp1675). VscN1 (ATPase) and VcrD1 (LcrD/SctV inner-membrane export ring) are additionally named with their Vsc/Vcr gene names in Nguyen 2020. VscF1 is the needle (Lian 2021). The translocon (VopB1/VP1657, VopD1/VP1656; Shimohata 2012) and the needle-tip LcrV homolog Vp1659/VP1659 (Zhou 2010) are counted as apparatus per the T3SS brief (translocon/tip proteins form the host-membrane pore and are machinery, not effectors). For the seven structural loci that the literature names only by role + vp number (vp1662, vp1670, vp1671, vp1672, vp1673, vp1674, vp1675, vp1690, vp1691, vp1695), the gene field carries the VP locus tag as the published identifier and the family column gives the Sct role; this is unavoidable because no Vsc/Vcr gene names were assigned to them in print. The export-gate block vp1662/vp1672-1675 maps to SctR/S/T/U but the paper does not split them per family, so each is listed once with the export-gate family. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: effectors VopQ/VP1680 (VopQ, vp1680), VopS/VP1686, VopR, VPA0226; the gatekeeper/regulatory-complex homologs vp1667/vp1665/vp1697/vp1666 (YopN/SycN/YscB/TyeA) were left out because the gatekeeper plus its chaperones blur into regulators and the paper presents them as a YopN-complex rather than naming a distinct SctW apparatus gene (a defensible low-risk omission; SctW could be added as vp1667/YopN if the benchmark wants the gatekeeper); transcriptional regulators ExsA (vp1699), ExsD (vp1698), ExsC, HlyU, H-NS, HU. Low-confidence calls: assigning VP1690/VP1695 to SctD vs SctJ and the export-block loci to specific SctR/S/T/U members is by homology family only, since the paper grouped rather than split them. All quotes are verbatim from the cited abstracts/full texts; all 7 DOIs and PMIDs were confirmed real via get_article_metadata.
